codeReview.summary: The implementation covers all required behavior: company-scoped autopilot enablement with settings persistence, versioned Product Program authoring with revision history, and cross-company isolation enforced at the entity lookup layer. The UI provides a settings form and Product Program editor with revision history display. All 15 tests pass and TypeScript compiles cleanly. No browser-visible verification was captured in the transcript skeleton, but AGENTS.md mandates browser verification for UI-visible features.

issues.description: listAutopilotProjectEntities does NOT filter by companyId, only by scopeId. The data handler for 'autopilot-projects' (line 180) applies a client-side filter, but the underlying entity query returns all companies' entities for a given scopeId. This is acceptable since the filter is applied, but a more robust approach would filter at the entity layer to avoid unnecessary data transfer.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider adding companyId filter to listAutopilotProjectEntities for consistency with the product-program-revision approach.

issues.description: findProductProgramRevision uses a server-side limit:100 and then filters in-memory. Combined with the client-side filter in listProductProgramRevisionEntities, this is functionally correct but the approach is redundant. For projects with many revisions, the in-memory limit could also cause missed revisions.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider using a cursor-based pagination or removing the artificial limit if the entity store supports it.

issues.description: AutopilotProjectSidebarLink only renders a text label, not a navigable link. The manifest registers it as a 'projectSidebarItem' but the component does not provide navigation behavior.
issues.suggestedFix: Either implement proper navigation to the Autopilot tab or remove the sidebar slot registration from manifest.ts.

issues.description: The saveAutopilotProject action merges with existing data but does not validate that required fields (companyId, projectId) remain unchanged after the merge. An accidental empty string passed for companyId could corrupt the entity.
issues.suggestedFix: Add invariant checks that companyId and projectId cannot be changed on an existing autopilot project.

issues.description: The saveProductProgramRevision action for existing revisions preserves version and createdAt (correct), but does not validate that the caller has permission to modify the revision beyond the companyId check. If the entity store has weak write-time validation, a caller could potentially overwrite revisions for a different projectId within the same company.
issues.suggestedFix: Add projectId validation in saveProductProgramRevision to ensure the revision belongs to the project being modified.

sharedStateObservations.description: The backend-worker skill mandates browser verification for UI-visible features. The transcript skeleton shows no agent-browser usage for this feature, even though the UI includes autopilot settings and Product Program editing. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true but the interactiveChecks array is empty, suggesting the browser step was skipped.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Backend-worker SKILL.md step 7: 'For every browser-visible behavior, verify it end-to-end with agent-browser'. Transcript skeleton shows no browser calls. handoff verification.interactiveChecks is empty.

sharedStateObservations.description: AGENTS.md requires browser validation for UI-visible flows. The feature has UI components (AutopilotSettings, ProductProgramEditor) but no browser verification was performed. This violates the stated convention even though skillFeedback reported followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md Testing & Validation Guidance: 'Browser-visible flows must be verified with agent-browser'. The autopilot UI surfaces include settings forms and revision history — these are browser-visible flows.

summary: The implementation is functionally complete and correct: all 15 tests pass covering VAL-AUTOPILOT-001 (enablement + settings persistence), VAL-AUTOPILOT-002 (revision creation/editing), VAL-AUTOPILOT-003 (versioned recoverable content), and VAL-CROSS-002 (company isolation). Cross-company access is denied at the entity lookup layer. Code quality is good with proper type validation, error handling, and input parsing. The main observation is that browser verification was not performed for the UI-visible settings and editor components, which AGENTS.md explicitly requires for any browser-visible feature. The skill feedback incorrectly reports followedProcedure=true despite the missing browser verification step.
